TF-E3.Summary: MVP 3: AI Intelligence — Multi-Agent Pipeline (Part 1: Kernel, MCP, Core Agents)
TF-E3.Description:
Deliver TaskFlow AI intelligence layer Part 1: AgentResultEnvelope, LangGraph scaffold, Agent OS Kernel, PostgreSQL MCP, Context and Planner agents, LLM router, spotlighting, InputSecurityScanner scaffold, memory foundation, and Tool Manager sandbox.

SCOPE (Part 1 — tasks TF-021 to TF-030):
- AgentResultEnvelope schema and TaskFlowGraphState
- Agent OS Kernel: scheduler, memory manager, tool manager scaffolds
- PostgreSQL MCP stdio client for read-only task/project context
- Context Agent: fetches tasks, projects, comments via MCP
- Planner Agent: NL → structured task, summaries, priority suggestions
- LLM Router with OpenAI/Anthropic primary and fallback
- Spotlighting utility wired to all external content
- InputSecurityScanner scaffold (regex layer only in Part 1)
- CoALA working + episodic memory foundation
- Tool Manager MCP sandbox with allowlists and timeouts

Part 2 (TF-E3-ai-intelligence-part2.json): Verification, Adversarial, Critic, Consensus, Orchestrator, full graph, AI API, prompts v2.0.0, caching, frontend.

EFFORT: XL (60 hours total across Part 1 + Part 2)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, AI Platform
DEPENDENCIES: TF-E2 complete — Redis, CRUD, search operational

EPIC LINK: epic/taskflow-implementation
FEATURE BRANCH: epic/TF-E3-ai-intelligence

REFERENCE DOCS:
- docs/TaskFlowAI_Project_Proposal.md (§ Multi-Agent Architecture, § Agent OS Kernel, § Memory)
- docs/MEMORY-ARCHITECTURE.md (when created)
- docs/AGENT-OS-KERNEL.md (when created)

SUCCESS CRITERIA (Part 1):
- Context Agent returns task list via MCP with RLS user_id enforced
- Planner Agent converts NL input to valid task JSON schema
- Spotlighting applied to all MCP responses before LLM
- Kernel Tool Manager enforces 30s timeout and 3-call chain limit
- Graph compiles with context → planner path (verification chain in Part 2)

Create Pydantic envelope schema and TaskFlowGraphState TypedDict for multi-agent communication.

USE CASES:
- Every agent node returns validated AgentResultEnvelope with trace_id
- LangGraph state carries user_id, workspace_id, NL request, and agent results map
- API layer deserializes final envelope for response formatting

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/schemas/envelope.py: AgentResultEnvelope, ExecutionMetadata, EscalationPayload
- Pydantic v2 ConfigDict(strict=True, frozen=True, extra='forbid')
- Fields: agent_id, canonical_role, status, result, metadata, escalation
- canonical_role Literal: tool_operator, planner, verifier, red_team, critic, supervisor
- Create backend/graph/state.py: TaskFlowGraphState TypedDict
- State fields: user_id, workspace_id, request_id, trace_id, nl_input, context_result, planner_result, verification_result, adversarial_result, critic_result, consensus_status, dlq_reason
- Create backend/graph/AGENT.md

EFFORT: S (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-003

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Valid envelope passes validation
- Invalid agent_id pattern raises ValidationError
- State TypedDict used in graph builder without mypy errors
- Frozen envelope immutable after creation

EDGE CASES:
- escalation.reason Literal matches proposal enum values
- tokens_used negative: ValidationError
- Missing trace_id: auto-generate UUID in node wrapper

Create backend/kernel/ with scheduler, memory manager, tool manager, identity manager, security monitor scaffolds.

USE CASES:
- All agent invocations route through kernel scheduler for ordering
- Tool Manager mediates every MCP call with sandbox limits
- Identity Manager attaches DelegationContext stub to agent calls
- Security Monitor records execution metrics per agent

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/kernel/__init__.py, backend/kernel/AGENT.md
- Create backend/kernel/scheduler.py: AgentScheduler.schedule(agent_id, priority) -> execution order
- Create backend/kernel/memory_manager.py: MemoryManager.get_working(), .distill_to_episodic() stubs
- Create backend/kernel/tool_manager.py: ToolManager.execute_tool(agent_id, tool, params) with allowlist check
- Create backend/kernel/identity_manager.py: IdentityManager.create_delegation_context(user_id, agent_id, intent) stub
- Create backend/kernel/security_monitor.py: SecurityMonitor.record_execution(agent_id, duration_ms, tokens)
- Wire kernel into graph builder as dependency injection

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, AI Platform
DEPENDENCIES: TF-021

TESTING CRITERIA:
- ToolManager rejects tool not in agent allowlist
- Scheduler returns parallel group [context_agent] before planner
- SecurityMonitor increments execution counter
- Kernel unit tests >=10 scenarios

EDGE CASES:
- Unknown agent_id in scheduler: raise KernelError
- Tool Manager timeout: raise MCPTimeoutError after 30s
- Identity manager without user_id: reject before MCP call

Create MCP client for read-only PostgreSQL access to tasks, projects, comments via stdio transport.

USE CASES:
- Context Agent lists tasks for current user via MCP query tool
- MCP client enforces user_id parameter on all queries (RLS defense in depth)
- Dev uses stdio MCP; tests use mock MCP client

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/mcp/__init__.py, backend/mcp/client.py: MCPClient base with call_tool()
- Create backend/mcp/stdio_transport.py: spawn @modelcontextprotocol/server-postgres via npx stdio
- Create backend/mcp/postgres_stdio.py: PostgresMCPClient with tools tasks.list, tasks.read, projects.list, comments.list
- Query validation: reject raw SQL without parameterized user_id binding
- Settings: MCP_TRANSPORT=stdio|mock, MCP_POSTGRES_URL, DATABASE_URL
- Integrate with ToolManager from kernel
- Document in docs/MCP.md

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-022, TF-005

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Mock transport: tasks.list returns seeded tasks
- Query without user_id rejected before MCP call
- Connection timeout 30s raises MCPTimeoutError
- HTTP legacy transport stub for unit tests unchanged

EDGE CASES:
- npx cold start: log latency warning, acceptable for MVP
- Empty task list: return [], not error
- MCP server crash: escalate with mcp_timeout reason
- SQL injection in filter params: parameterized queries only

Create Context Agent LangGraph node that fetches task/project context via PostgreSQL MCP.

USE CASES:
- AI pipeline needs current tasks, project metadata, and recent comments for planning
- Context Agent runs in parallel with project metadata fetch (generator phase)
- All MCP data spotlighted before inclusion in graph state

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/context/__init__.py, AGENT.md, node.py
- context_agent_node(state) -> AgentResultEnvelope
- Call MCP via ToolManager: tasks.list, projects.list, comments.list for workspace
- Apply spotlighting to all text fields in MCP responses
- Sort tasks by priority and due_date; limit to 50 most relevant
- Return result: {tasks, projects, comments, context_summary}
- Handle MCP timeout: status=escalated, reason=mcp_timeout

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-023, TF-021

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Node returns valid AgentResultEnvelope
- Spotlighting markers present on all external strings in result
- Empty workspace returns success with empty lists
- MCP timeout triggers escalation

EDGE CASES:
- Partial MCP failure: include available data, flag partial in metadata
- >50 tasks: truncate with truncated=true flag
- Comments on inaccessible tasks: filtered by RLS at MCP layer
- PII in task description: passed through spotlighted, not logged raw

Create LLM router supporting Claude Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5, GPT-4o-mini fallback with token budget tracking.

USE CASES:
- Planner Agent calls LLM with reasoning_effort=high for NL → task parsing
- Primary provider rate limited → automatic fallback to secondary
- PII-classified request forces local LLM when enabled (MVP 5 full wiring)
- Token usage tracked per agent for circuit breaker

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/llm/__init__.py, backend/llm/router.py: LLMRouter class
- Providers: Anthropic (claude-opus-4-8), OpenAI (gpt-5.5), fallback gpt-4o-mini
- Methods: generate(messages, model, max_tokens, reasoning_effort, effort) -> LLMResponse
- Token budget per agent from settings; circuit break at 2x budget
- Prompt caching: cache_control blocks for Claude system prompts (stable content)
- Return LLMResponse: content, model_used, tokens_input, tokens_output, cache_read_tokens, latency_ms
- Settings: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, LLM_PRIMARY_PROVIDER

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-003

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Mock provider returns parsed response
- 429 from primary triggers fallback
- Token budget exceeded raises TokenBudgetExceededError
- Cache read tokens reported when cache hit

EDGE CASES:
- Both providers fail: raise LLMError, route to DLQ
- Empty messages list: ValueError before API call
- max_tokens=0: ValueError
- Timeout 60s: retry once then escalate

Create Planner Agent for NL → structured task, project summaries, and priority suggestions.

USE CASES:
- User types 'Add high-priority bug fix for login timeout due Friday' → structured task JSON
- User requests sprint summary for project → bullet summary of progress
- User asks 'What should I work on today?' → prioritized task list with reasoning

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/planner/__init__.py, AGENT.md, node.py
- Load prompts from prompts/planner/ (11-file structure started, completed in TF-038)
- Input: spotlighted context from Context Agent + user NL request
- Run InputSecurityScanner (regex layer) before LLM call
- Output schema: PlannerResult with mode (create_task|summary|prioritize), task_draft, summary, priorities
- Call LLMRouter with reasoning_effort=high (GPT-5.5) or effort=xhigh (Claude)
- Parse LLM JSON output with Pydantic validation; 1 retry on schema failure

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-024, TF-025, TF-027, TF-028

TESTING CRITERIA:
- NL create task returns valid task_draft with title, priority, due_date
- Summary mode returns non-empty summary string
- Injection in NL input blocked by scanner before LLM
- Invalid LLM JSON triggers one retry then escalation

EDGE CASES:
- Ambiguous due date 'next Friday': resolve relative to user timezone from settings
- No tasks in context for prioritize mode: return onboarding suggestions
- Token budget exceeded: escalate, no partial task created
- LLM returns markdown wrapper around JSON: strip and parse

Implement and wire spotlighting for all user-generated and MCP-sourced content before LLM processing.

USE CASES:
- Task title containing 'ignore previous instructions' treated as data not commands
- MCP response wrapped before passing to Planner Agent
- System prompts include CRITICAL SECURITY RULE for external content markers

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Expand backend/security/spotlighting.py from MVP 1 scaffold
- Functions: spotlight_external_content(str) -> str, spotlight_dict(data, text_fields) -> dict
- Markers: <<<EXTERNAL_CONTENT>>> ... <<</EXTERNAL_CONTENT>>>
- Integrate in: context agent node, planner input builder, comment/task service AI prep
- Add is_spotlighted(content) check for double-wrapping prevention
- Tests: backend/tests/security/test_spotlighting.py with injection payloads

EFFORT: S (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-011

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Content wrapped exactly once
- Already spotlighted content not double-wrapped
- Nested markers in user input escaped or preserved safely
- metadata.spotlighting_applied=true on agent envelopes

EDGE CASES:
- Empty string: return empty without markers
- Very long content (>100KB): spotlight without truncation
- Unicode and emoji in content: preserve encoding
- Binary attachment metadata: skip spotlighting on non-text fields

Create InputSecurityScanner with PromptInjectionDetector regex layer; ML and constitutional layers in TF-041.

USE CASES:
- Known jailbreak patterns in task description blocked before LLM call
- Clean legitimate task text passes without false positive
- Blocked input routes graph to DLQ with security_violation_detected

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/security/injection.py: PromptInjectionDetector with pattern list
- Patterns: ignore previous, disregard training, DAN mode, [[SYSTEM]], <|im_start|>, ```system
- NFKC normalization, base64/hex decode attempts before match
- Create backend/security/input_scanner.py: InputSecurityScanner.scan(text) -> ScanResult
- Phase 1: regex only; stub hooks for PromptGuard and constitutional (MVP 4)
- Integrate in planner node and future API AI endpoints
- Log detections to security logger with trace_id

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-027

TESTING CRITERIA:
- OWASP sample payloads from jailbreak_corpus.yaml (subset) blocked
- False positive rate <1% on clean task descriptions corpus
- ScanResult includes layer=regex, matched_pattern, confidence
- Blocked scan raises SecurityViolationError caught by graph

EDGE CASES:
- Case insensitive matching
- Patterns split across lines with whitespace
- Unicode homoglyph bypass: NFKC normalization
- Scanner disabled via LLAMAFIREWALL_ENABLED=false skips ML only, not regex

Implement working memory in Redis and episodic memory in PostgreSQL with session isolation.

USE CASES:
- AI session stores active graph state in working memory (Redis TTL session)
- Successful prioritization patterns distilled to episodic memory for future sessions
- User A cannot read user B's episodic memory (namespace isolation)

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/memory/__init__.py, AGENT.md, working.py, episodic.py
- WorkingMemory: Redis keys taskflow:wm:{session_id} with LangGraph state snapshot, TTL 1h
- EpisodicMemory: PostgreSQL episodic_entries table — user_id, session_id, lesson_type, content, version
- MemoryManager.distill_to_episodic() called after successful planner run
- Session namespace: {user_id}:{workspace_id}:{session_id}
- Alembic migration 004_episodic_memory
- Document in docs/MEMORY-ARCHITECTURE.md

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-022, TF-018

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Working memory roundtrip save/load state
- Episodic entry isolated per user_id
- Cross-user read returns empty
- Memory read logged in audit trail

EDGE CASES:
- Redis unavailable: degrade to in-memory working store for single instance
- Episodic content spotlighted on retrieval
- Session expired: working memory miss, start fresh
- Distill on failed planner run: skip episodic write

Harden Tool Manager with process isolation config, allowlists, chaining limits, and MCPResponseValidator stub.

USE CASES:
- Context Agent limited to 3 MCP calls per invocation
- Disallowed tool calendar.read blocked even if agent requests it
- MCP response validated against Pydantic schema before returning to agent

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Expand backend/kernel/tool_manager.py
- Allowlist JSON per agent_id in settings or config/tool_allowlist.json
- Enforce max 3 sequential tool calls per agent invocation (counter in state)
- Create backend/mcp/validator.py: MCPResponseValidator.validate(tool, response) — schema layer only
- TaskListSchema, ProjectListSchema Pydantic models for MCP responses
- Log every call: {user_id, agent_id, tool, latency_ms, trace_id, response_size}
- Resource limits documented for supervisord MCP processes (512MB, 30s timeout)

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-023, TF-022

TESTING CRITERIA:
- 4th tool call in chain raises ToolChainLimitExceeded
- Tool not in allowlist raises ToolNotAllowedError
- Invalid MCP response schema raises MCPValidationError
- MCP call log entry created for each call

EDGE CASES:
- Tool-to-tool direct call forbidden: must go through agent node
- Validator on empty response: return valid empty list schema
- Oversized response >1MB: quarantine flag, truncate with warning
- Concurrent tool calls from parallel agents: separate counters per agent_id
